name: Kurdus ibn Zuhayr
title: Lion of Taghlib
shortDescription: The brave brother of Qasit and Muqsit who reached Karbala under the cover of night and was martyred in the first attack.
fullStory:
Kurdus ibn Zuhayr al-Taghlibi was another valiant Shia and loyal companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) in Kufa. Having learned bravery fighting alongside his master in the battles of Siffin and Nahrawan, he immediately answered the call of truth during Imam Hussain's (AS) uprising.

He, along with his two brothers, Qasit and Muqsit, realized that Ibn Ziyad had blocked the exit routes from Kufa. However, their love for the Master of Martyrs gave them the courage to secretly break through the siege in the dark of night and head towards Karbala.

Kurdus and his brothers reached Imam Hussain's (AS) camp on the eighth of Muharram. On Ashura, as the shower of arrows and the enemy's all-out attack began in the morning, Kurdus drew his sword like a roaring lion.

He fought on the front lines alongside his brothers and, after taking down a number of enemies, drank the cup of martyrdom in that very first attack, proudly meeting his Lord.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى قاسِطٍ وَكُرْدُوسٍ ابْنَيْ زُهَيْرٍ التَّغْلِبِيَّيْنِ
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Qasit and Kurdus, the sons of Zuhayr al-Taghlibi.
